what: Initialize the .nori data directory inside the project with settings.json and .gitignore
why: Every registered project needs a local .nori directory to store project settings (ID, connected vaults) and keep logs out of version control

output.console: Path to the created .nori directory and its settings
output.file: Creates .nori/settings.json and .nori/.gitignore inside the project directory

success_handling.criteria: .nori directory exists (created or already present), settings.json is written or read successfully, and .gitignore is in place

error_handling.criteria: fs.mkdirSync throws due to permissions or disk errors
error_handling.action: Return error indicating the .nori directory could not be created

error_handling.criteria: fs.writeFileSync throws when writing settings.json or .gitignore
error_handling.action: Return error indicating settings could not be written to disk

error_handling.criteria: JSON.parse fails on an existing settings.json during re-registration
error_handling.action: Silently overwrite the corrupted file with fresh settings

event.message: Corrupted settings.json overwritten with fresh settings

decisions.condition: .nori directory and settings.json created or read successfully
decisions.outcome: Proceed to save-to-db step with project settings
Read existing settings and reuse them, proceed to save-to-db step
Abort registration, return error to user
